summary: Frontend-only RoomPage layout regression validation completed on public preview URL. Requested desktop behavior passed: participants card stayed controlled at 2 visible users + overflow CTA, timer remained visible in left column without being pushed below fold, participants modal behavior stayed intact, chat kept internal scroll, and timer controls remained usable.

issues: Accessibility warning in console: DialogContent renders without Description/aria-describedby (Radix warning).

action_items: Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to participants modal DialogContent to remove accessibility warning.
Optional: add a stable frontend e2e regression test file for this RoomPage left-column layout scenario.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/src/pages/RoomPage.js is 987 lines (>700 guideline); split participants, timer, and chat into smaller components to reduce regression risk in layout-only changes.
test_credentials: Read /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Dev preview auth bypass user: test@izlek.dev (MOCKED auth mode).
seed_data_creation: Created one test room via public API (room_id: dd6f784f-a562-42c9-ad3b-d482b9f73636, code: 2C005C), added 3 synthetic participants to force overflow CTA, and seeded 15 room messages for chat scroll validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on desktop viewport 1920x1080 against public URL from frontend/.env. Measured bounding boxes confirm timer card stayed inside viewport while participants card remained compact. Page-level scroll overflow was false while chat viewport overflow was true, matching requested behavior.
rca of the issue: No regression reproduced for the requested RoomPage layout fix. Observed warning RCA: participants modal DialogContent is missing a description binding (aria-describedby), which triggers Radix accessibility warning but does not break functionality.
